details.impact: The assisted import mapping UI may be unstable or too brittle to reach the expected confirmation preview.
details.reproduction:
1. Open /app/imports
2. Upload the assisted appointments CSV fixture
3. Observe whether the mapping preview and confirmation CTA appear

bugs.title: Assisted import preview did not stabilize in browser QA

details.impact: The operational layer would stop being trustworthy because the same imported base would no longer classify into the expected confirmation/reminder/at-risk/recovery/review buckets.
details.reproduction:
1. Seed the stage 10 operational dataset
2. Run the classification services for the workspace
3. Compare the resulting counts against the expected seeded cases

reasons.description: The appointment is already inside the reminder window, but REVORY has no usable email for the client.
reasons.label: Reminder blocked by missing email

categoryCards.description: 1 need immediate attention and 0 stay on the watchlist.
categoryCards.nextAction: Review these appointments first. REVORY is surfacing explainable signals, not predictive scoring.
categoryCards.title: At-risk appointments

categoryCards.description: 2 appointments are already inside the 48h confirmation window.
categoryCards.nextAction: 1 still need a usable email before confirmation can help.
categoryCards.title: Confirmation queue

categoryCards.description: 1 appointments are already inside the 24h reminder window.
categoryCards.nextAction: 1 still need a usable email before reminder logic can help.
categoryCards.title: Reminder queue

categoryCards.description: 1 recent disruptions were surfaced without pretending there is already a rebooking machine behind them.
categoryCards.nextAction: Keep recovery visible as guided opportunity, not as a call-center flow.
categoryCards.title: Recovery opportunities

categoryCards.description: 1 completed visits are already eligible for a simple review request layer.
categoryCards.nextAction: Keep reviews honest: this is eligibility visibility, not a live campaign.
categoryCards.title: Review-ready visits

priorityItems.nextAction: Add a usable client email so REVORY can support the next confirmation or reminder step.
Keep this appointment visible in the narrow reminder-ready queue.
Keep this appointment visible in the confirmation-ready queue.
Keep this disrupted visit visible for the first recovery outreach layer.

priorityItems.insight: Completed recently and eligible for a review request
priorityItems.nextAction: Keep this visit visible for the first email-first review layer.

prioritySummary.description: REVORY has already surfaced a small group of appointments that deserves a closer look now. The short list below follows priority order: at-risk first, then reminder, confirmation, recovery, and review visibility.
prioritySummary.headline: A small set of appointments needs attention now.
prioritySummary.suggestedNextAction: Start with the blocked contact paths, then review the tight-window appointments while there is still time to protect the slot.

reasons.description: The appointment was canceled and there is no later scheduled visit in the current workspace.
reasons.label: Canceled without rebooking

reasons.description: The appointment was completed recently enough to qualify for a simple post-visit review request in the MVP.
reasons.label: Completed recently

bugs.title: Sprint 4 classification counts diverged from the seeded operational dataset

details.impact: The operational layer would be technically computed but functionally invisible or ambiguous to the MedSpa user.
details.reproduction:
1. Import the stage 10 appointments dataset
2. Open /app/dashboard
3. Inspect the Operational Layer section and the priority list
details.textSnippet: REVORY MEDSPA REVENUE RECOVERY WORKSPACE Dashboard Imports & Mapping REVENUE Active Flows Empty Slots GROWTH Reviews Recall ACCOUNT Activation Setup S4 Sprint 4 QA Workspace ACTIVE ACTIVE WORKSPACE ACTIVE revory-sprint4-etapa10-1774642798556@example.com Sprint 4 QA Workspace Revenue recovery workspace MedSpa-first Mode B Activated ACTIVE R OPERATOR revory-sprint4-etapa10-1774642798556@example.com Sign out OPERATIONS OVERVIEW Your workspace already has an operational base. Imported appointments and clients already give REVORY enough structure to keep the operational layer readable. Confirmation, recovery, and review execution can build from this base. Mar 2026 Operational base live CURRENT ACCESS POINT Open imports whenever you need to refresh the base or review import quality. Open imports MONITORED REVENUE BASE $1,290.00 Current revenue already attached to imported appointments. APPOINTMENTS MONITORED 7 Appointments already persisted in this workspace. UPCOMING APPOINTMENTS 5 Scheduled ahead of the current server time. CLIENT PROFILES 8 Current stored client base available to REVORY. CANCELLED APPOINTMENTS 1 Imported rows already marked as canceled. OPERATIONAL LAYER Who needs action, and why. A narrow operating layer that keeps signal, current status, and the next suggested action readable without pretending REVORY is already a CRM or an automation engine. Signals live OPERATIONAL FOCUS A small set of appointments needs attention now. 1 attention now Updated Mar 27, 5:21 PM REVORY has already surfaced a small group of appointments that deserves a closer look now. The short list below follows priority order: at-risk first, then reminder, confirmation, recovery, and review visibility. SIGNALS SURFACED 5 Guided items currently visible in the workspace. ATTENTION NOW 1 Tight-window appointments surfaced by the current logic. OPERATIONAL FRICTION 1 blocked Visible, but still secondary to live action queues. CURRENT PRIORITY Start with the blocked contact paths, then review the tight-window appointments while there is still time to protect the slot. Priority signal 1 blocked At-risk appointments 1 need immediate attention and 0 stay on the watchlist. 1 CURRENT GUIDANCE Review these appointments first. REVORY is surfacing explainable signals, not predictive scoring. Ready queue 1 blocked Confirmation queue 2 appointments are already inside the 48h confirmation window. 2 CURRENT GUIDANCE 1 still need a usable email before confirmation can help. Ready queue 1 blocked Reminder queue 1 appointments are already inside the 24h reminder window. 1 CURRENT GUIDANCE 1 still need a usable email before reminder logic can help. Opportunity Recovery opportunities 1 recent disruptions were surfaced without pretending there is already a rebooking machine behind them. 1 CURRENT GUIDANCE Keep recovery visible as guided opportunity, not as a call-center flow. Eligibility Review-ready visits 1 completed visits are already eligible for a simple review request layer. 1 CURRENT GUIDANCE Keep reviews honest: this is eligibility visibility, not a live campaign. Operational focus list This is a short, prioritized list. REVORY is not trying to become a CRM inbox here. 5 items surfaced Priority follows this reading order: at-risk, reminder, confirmation, recovery, then review visibility. Lower-priority categories stay visible in their cards even when they do not lead the short list. Reminder Reminder ready Noa Reminder Skin Booster - Dr. Maia TIMING Scheduled Mar 28, 5:19 AM $220.00 WHY SURFACED Inside reminder window CURRENT STATUS Reminder ready RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP Keep this appointment visible in the narrow reminder-ready queue. Confirmation Confirmation ready Mia Confirmation Hydrafacial - Dr. Maia TIMING Scheduled Mar 29, 5:19 AM $180.00 WHY SURFACED Inside confirmation window CURRENT STATUS Confirmation ready RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP Keep this appointment visible in the confirmation-ready queue. Recovery Recovery ready Rafa Recovery Botox - Dr. Maia TIMING Disrupted Mar 25, 5:19 PM $300.00 WHY SURFACED Canceled without rebooking CURRENT STATUS Recovery ready RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP Keep this disrupted visit visible for the first recovery outreach layer. Reviews Review-ready Bia Review Peeling - Dr. Maia TIMING Completed Mar 25, 5:19 PM $140.00 WHY SURFACED Completed recently and eligible for a review request CURRENT STATUS Review-ready RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP Keep this visit visible for the first email-first review layer.
